title: Pachypteris densiflora
taxonomy: Pachypteris densiflora is classified as a species of extinct plant in the family Matoniaceae, which is a group of extinct ferns. This plant is known to have lived during the Carboniferous period, approximately 320-300 million years ago. As a member of the Matoniaceae family, Pachypteris densiflora shares characteristics with other extinct ferns. The exact classification of this plant may vary depending on the source, but it is widely recognized as a significant fossil discovery in the field of paleobotany. Further study and analysis of Pachypteris densiflora can provide more insight into its classification and evolutionary relationships.
uses: Pachypteris densiflora, also known as the dense-flowered Pachypteris, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. However, no specific information is available about its medicinal uses.
parts: Unfortunately, I was unable to find information regarding the medicinal parts of Pachypteris densiflora.
